questions.question: If the polynomial $p(x) = x^4 - 6x^3 + 16x^2 - 25x + 10$ is divided by another polynomial $g(x) = x^2 - 2x + k$, the remainder is found to be of the form $x + a$. Find the values of $k$ and $a$.

steps: Perform long division of $p(x)$ by $g(x) = x^2 - 2x + k$.
Multiply $x^2$ by $(x^2 - 2x + k)$ to subtract from $p(x)$, then repeat for $-4x$ and $(4-k)$.
Equate the calculated remainder expression $(4k - 16 + 25)x + (10 - k(4 - k))$ to the given remainder $(x + a)$.
Solve the resulting linear equations by comparing coefficients of $x$ and the constant terms.
solution.derivation: x^4 - 6x^3 + 16x^2 - 25x + 10 = (x^2 - 2x + k)(x^2 - 4x + (8-k)) + ( (4k - 16 + 25)x + (10 - k(8-k)) ) \\ (4k - 16 + 25)x + (10 - 8k + k^2) = x + a \\ 4k + 9 = 1 \Rightarrow 4k = -8 \Rightarrow k = -2 \text{ (Correction: check division coefficients)} \\ \text{Corrected division: } x^2(x^2 - 2x + k) = x^4 - 2x^3 + kx^2 \\ \text{Remainder after subtraction: } -4x^3 + (16-k)x^2 - 25x + 10 \\ -4x(x^2 - 2x + k) = -4x^3 + 8x^2 - 4kx \\ \text{Remainder after subtraction: } (8-k)x^2 + (4k-25)x + 10 \\ (8-k)(x^2 - 2x + k) = (8-k)x^2 - 2(8-k)x + k(8-k) \\ \text{Final Remainder: } (4k - 25 + 16 - 2k)x + (10 - 8k + k^2) = (2k - 9)x + (k^2 - 8k + 10) \\ 2k - 9 = 1 \Rightarrow 2k = 10 \Rightarrow k = 5 \\ a = k^2 - 8k + 10 = 25 - 40 + 10 = -5
